name: File Creation by Non-Privileged Processes in Program Files Directory
notes:
This rule detects file creation by non-privileged processes in the Program Files directory.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Ryan Plas (rule), oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Files Dropped to Program Files by Non-Priviledged Process
SIGMA Description: Search for dropping of files to Windows/Program Files fodlers by non-priviledged processes


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, System.File Created

when the event matches LOWER("Integrity Level") == 'medium' AND ( ( LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\program files\%' OR LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\program files (x86)\%' ) OR ( LOWER("Filename") LIKE '\windows\%' AND LOWER("Filename") NOT LIKE '%temp%' ) ) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY File Creation by Non-Privileged Processes in Program Files Directory on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, System.File Created

AND when the event matches LOWER("Integrity Level") == 'medium' AND (     (         LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\program files\%'         OR LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%\program files (x86)\%'     )     OR      (         LOWER("Filename") LIKE '\windows\%'         AND LOWER("Filename") NOT LIKE '%temp%'     ) ) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: File Creation by Non-Privileged Processes in Program Files Directory
Event description: Detects file creation by non-privileged processes in the Program Files directory.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
